entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A leguminous, cool-season food crop cultivated prevalently in the Asian Pacific region.

entities.name: ASIAN PACIFIC REGION
entities.description: The region where chickpea is prevalently cultivated.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach that has resulted in several important improvements in chickpea.

entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGY
entities.description: A field of science including plant tissue culture and genetic transformation to improve crop sustainability.

entities.name: PLANT TISSUE CULTURE
entities.description: A biotechnological method contributing to the sustainability of chickpea.

entities.name: GENETIC TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: A biotechnological method used to create transgenic plants in chickpea.

entities.name: AGROBACTERIUM-MEDIATED TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: An efficient protocol for genetic transformation of chickpea using axillary meristem explants.

entities.name: AGROBACTERIUM
entities.description: The bacterium used as a vector for the transformation protocol.

entities.name: AXILLARY MERISTEM EXPLANTS
entities.description: The specific plant tissue used in the Agrobacterium-mediated transformation protocol.

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The goal of applying biotechnology to food crops like chickpea.
